definition: Existing in very large amounts; more than enough.
exampleSentence: The garden had an abundant supply of tomatoes this summer.

definition: To succeed in doing something, especially something that takes effort.
exampleSentence: She worked hard to accomplish her goal of reading fifty books in one year.

definition: Correct and exact, without any mistakes.
exampleSentence: The scientist made sure her measurements were accurate before recording them.

definition: To change or adjust in order to fit a new situation.
exampleSentence: Animals must adapt to their environment to survive.

definition: Very old; from a time long ago in history.
exampleSentence: The museum displayed ancient pottery from thousands of years ago.

definition: Feeling worried or nervous about something.
exampleSentence: Marcus felt anxious about his first day at the new school.

definition: Easy to see or understand; obvious.
exampleSentence: It was apparent from her smile that she had good news to share.

definition: Close to an exact amount but not completely exact.
exampleSentence: The drive from home to school takes approximately twenty minutes.

definition: To put things in a particular order or to plan something.
exampleSentence: The librarian helped arrange the books on the shelf by author name.

definition: To believe something is true without checking the facts.
exampleSentence: Don't assume the answer is wrong just because it looks different.

definition: The layer of gases surrounding a planet, or the mood of a place.
exampleSentence: Earth's atmosphere protects us from harmful rays from the sun.

definition: Something good that helps or improves a situation.
exampleSentence: One benefit of reading every day is that your vocabulary grows.

definition: A line or limit that separates one area from another.
exampleSentence: The river forms a natural boundary between the two towns.

definition: Having the skill or ability to do something well.
exampleSentence: She is a very capable student who always finishes her work on time.

definition: Being careful to avoid danger or mistakes.
exampleSentence: The cautious hiker checked the trail map before setting out.

definition: A feature or quality that makes someone or something recognizable.
exampleSentence: Stripes are a key characteristic of zebras.

definition: An organized society with its own culture, government, and way of life.
exampleSentence: The ancient Egyptian civilization built the pyramids thousands of years ago.

definition: The usual weather conditions in a particular area over a long time.
exampleSentence: The tropical climate near the equator is warm and rainy year-round.

definition: To fall down or break apart suddenly.
exampleSentence: The old bridge began to collapse after years without repairs.

definition: To look at two or more things to find how they are alike or different.
exampleSentence: The teacher asked us to compare the two stories and find three similarities.

definition: To reach a decision or opinion after thinking about the facts.
exampleSentence: Based on the clues in the story, we can conclude that the character is brave.

definition: A struggle or disagreement between two sides.
exampleSentence: The conflict between the two friends was resolved when they talked it out.

definition: A result or effect that happens because of something else.
exampleSentence: One consequence of not studying is doing poorly on the test.

definition: Happening all the time or never changing.
exampleSentence: The constant rain made it impossible to play outside all week.

definition: To build or put something together.
exampleSentence: The workers will construct a new playground in the park this summer.

definition: To give or add something to help a group or cause.
exampleSentence: Every student can contribute ideas during a class discussion.

definition: To persuade someone to believe or do something.
exampleSentence: She tried to convince her parents to let her adopt a kitten.

definition: Brave enough to do something that is difficult or scary.
exampleSentence: The courageous firefighter ran into the burning building to help.

definition: Wanting to learn or know more about something.
exampleSentence: The curious child asked dozens of questions about how rockets work.

definition: A formal discussion where people argue different sides of a topic.
exampleSentence: The class held a debate about whether students should wear uniforms.

definition: To become smaller, fewer, or weaker over time.
exampleSentence: The number of butterflies in the area has started to decline.

definition: To show clearly how something works or how to do it.
exampleSentence: The teacher will demonstrate how to solve the math problem step by step.

definition: To rely on someone or something for support or help.
exampleSentence: Baby birds depend on their parents for food until they can fly.

definition: To explain what something is like using words.
exampleSentence: Can you describe what the main character looks like in the story?

definition: To figure out or decide something after careful thought.
exampleSentence: Scientists must determine whether the experiment's results are reliable.

definition: To grow or change over time into something more complete.
exampleSentence: A caterpillar will develop into a butterfly over several weeks.

definition: To find or learn about something for the first time.
exampleSentence: Marie Curie helped discover the element radium.

definition: To notice the differences between two or more things.
exampleSentence: It can be hard to distinguish between a frog and a toad at first glance.

definition: A long period of time with very little or no rain.
exampleSentence: The drought caused the lake to dry up almost completely.

definition: Very excited and wanting to do something right away.
exampleSentence: The eager students raised their hands before the teacher finished the question.

definition: A change that happens because of a cause or action.
exampleSentence: One effect of pollution is that the air becomes harder to breathe.

definition: To give more details or explain something more fully.
exampleSentence: The author could elaborate on the character's feelings to make the story richer.

definition: To come out from a hidden or enclosed place.
exampleSentence: The butterfly will emerge from its cocoon when it is ready.

definition: To give someone support or confidence to do something.
exampleSentence: Good coaches encourage their players even after a tough loss.

definition: Extremely large in size or amount.
exampleSentence: The enormous whale was longer than a school bus.

definition: The natural world around us, including air, water, and land.
exampleSentence: Recycling is one way we can help protect the environment.

definition: Absolutely necessary; something you cannot do without.
exampleSentence: Water is essential for all living things to survive.

definition: To set up or create something that is meant to last.
exampleSentence: The colonists worked to establish a new town near the river.

definition: To make a careful guess about an amount or value.
exampleSentence: Can you estimate how many jelly beans are in the jar?

definition: Facts or details that help prove something is true.
exampleSentence: The detective found evidence that helped solve the mystery.

definition: To look at something very carefully to learn more about it.
exampleSentence: The scientist used a microscope to examine the tiny cells.

definition: To go beyond or be greater than a limit or expectation.
exampleSentence: Her test score will exceed the class average if she studies hard.

definition: To give something and receive something else in return.
exampleSentence: The two pen pals exchange letters every month.

definition: A test done to learn something new or check if an idea is correct.
exampleSentence: We designed an experiment to find out which soil helps plants grow fastest.

definition: To make something clear so that others can understand it.
exampleSentence: The teacher asked the student to explain how she got her answer.

definition: Well known because you have seen or experienced it before.
exampleSentence: The song sounded familiar, but she couldn't remember the title.

definition: An important or noticeable part of something.
exampleSentence: One feature of a desert is that it receives very little rainfall.

definition: Able to bend easily or willing to change plans.
exampleSentence: A flexible schedule allows the family to take trips on different days.

definition: Happening often or many times.
exampleSentence: Frequent practice is the best way to improve at playing the piano.

definition: All the people born and living around the same time period.
exampleSentence: Each generation brings new ideas and inventions to the world.

definition: Real and true; not fake or pretend.
exampleSentence: Her smile was genuine because she was truly happy to see her friend.

definition: Happening slowly, little by little over time.
exampleSentence: The sky gradually turned from blue to orange as the sun set.

definition: The natural home or living place of an animal or plant.
exampleSentence: A coral reef is the habitat for thousands of different fish species.

definition: To pause before doing something because you feel uncertain.
exampleSentence: She didn't hesitate to raise her hand when the teacher asked for volunteers.

definition: To recognize or figure out what something is.
exampleSentence: Can you identify the main idea of this paragraph?

definition: To explain or make something clear by using examples or pictures.
exampleSentence: The diagram helps illustrate how the water cycle works.

definition: A strong effect or influence on something.
exampleSentence: The new recycling program had a big impact on reducing waste in the school.

definition: To show or point out something.
exampleSentence: Dark clouds indicate that a storm might be on the way.

definition: A single person, separate from others in a group.
exampleSentence: Each individual on the team has a special role to play.

definition: The power to change or affect someone's actions or thoughts.
exampleSentence: Her older sister was a big influence on her love of reading.

definition: To live in a particular place.
exampleSentence: Many different species of birds inhabit the tropical rainforest.

definition: To carefully study or examine something to learn the truth.
exampleSentence: The students decided to investigate why the plants near the window grew taller.

definition: To give good reasons to explain why something is right.
exampleSentence: You must justify your answer by showing your work on the math problem.

definition: To find where something is.
exampleSentence: Use the map to locate the capital city of each state.

definition: Extremely beautiful or impressive.
exampleSentence: The view from the mountaintop was absolutely magnificent.

definition: More than half of a group or total.
exampleSentence: The majority of students voted for pizza at the school lunch poll.

definition: A planned way of doing something.
exampleSentence: The scientific method helps researchers test their ideas carefully.

definition: To move from one place to another, usually with the seasons.
exampleSentence: Geese migrate south every fall to find warmer weather.

definition: Slightly wet; damp.
exampleSentence: The soil needs to stay moist for the seeds to sprout.

definition: A story or account of events.
exampleSentence: The author wrote a narrative about a family's journey across the country.

definition: Needed; something that must be done or had.
exampleSentence: It is necessary to wear a helmet when riding a bicycle.

definition: To watch something carefully to learn from it.
exampleSentence: Scientists observe animals in the wild to learn about their behavior.

definition: To get something, especially through effort.
exampleSentence: You can obtain a library card by signing up at the front desk.

definition: To happen or take place.
exampleSentence: Earthquakes occur when pieces of the earth's surface suddenly shift.

definition: To disagree with or act against something.
exampleSentence: Some citizens opposed the plan to build a highway through the park.

definition: To arrange things in a neat or logical order.
exampleSentence: She likes to organize her notes by color before studying for a test.

definition: The place or situation where something begins.
exampleSentence: The origin of the tradition can be traced back hundreds of years.

definition: A section of a written work, or a way through from one place to another.
exampleSentence: Read the passage carefully before answering the questions.

definition: To keep going even when something is difficult.
exampleSentence: If you persist in practicing, you will eventually learn to play the song.

definition: To cause someone to do or believe something through reasoning.
exampleSentence: The essay tried to persuade readers to recycle more often.

definition: To say what you think will happen in the future.
exampleSentence: Based on the clues in the story, can you predict what will happen next?

definition: To keep something safe and in good condition.
exampleSentence: National parks help preserve natural land for future generations.

definition: Happening or existing before the current one.
exampleSentence: In the previous chapter, the hero found the hidden map.

definition: A series of steps or actions taken to complete something.
exampleSentence: Photosynthesis is the process plants use to make food from sunlight.

definition: To make or create something.
exampleSentence: Factories produce goods that people buy in stores.

definition: Not found or seen very often; uncommon.
exampleSentence: Seeing a snow leopard in the wild is extremely rare.

definition: To suggest something as a good choice.
exampleSentence: I recommend this book because it has an exciting plot and interesting characters.

definition: A large area of land that has common features.
exampleSentence: The desert region receives less than ten inches of rain each year.

definition: To stand for or be a symbol of something.
exampleSentence: The stars on the flag represent the fifty states.

definition: To need something or make something necessary.
exampleSentence: Most plants require sunlight and water to grow.

definition: To look like or be similar to something else.
exampleSentence: The cloud formations resemble animals if you use your imagination.

definition: Something useful that can be used to accomplish a goal.
exampleSentence: Water is one of our most important natural resources.

definition: To show or make known something that was hidden.
exampleSentence: The last chapter will reveal the identity of the mysterious stranger.

definition: A particular order in which things follow each other.
exampleSentence: Put the events of the story in the correct sequence from first to last.

definition: Important enough to have an effect or be noticed.
exampleSentence: The invention of the printing press was a significant moment in history.
